title: prunus × lannesiana f. plenapendula
intro: Prunus × lannesiana f. plenapendula, commonly known as the double-flowered cherry, is a cultivar of flowering plant that belongs to the Rosaceae family. This plant is known for its stunning double-petaled flowers and is often used as an ornamental in gardens and parks. The double-flowered cherry has been used in traditional medicine to alleviate various health issues, with its health benefits attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and prevent infections. The bioactive constituents of Prunus × lannesiana f. plenapendula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic actions, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects.
benefits: Prunus × lannesiana f. plenapendula has many benefits, such as its ability to promote physical health through the presence of antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and improve heart health, lower the risk of certain cancers, and even alleviate symptoms of osteoporosis and Alzheimer's disease. This flowering plant also has a positive impact on mental well-being by releasing fragrances and pollens that can reduce stress and anxiety levels, while its nectar-rich flowers attract pollinators, supporting local ecosystems and biodiversity. Furthermore, Prunus × lannesiana f. plenapendula has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, such as fever, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. By incorporating this plant into one's life, individuals can experience improved overall health, increased happiness, and a deeper connection with nature.
parts: Prunus × lannesiana f. plenapendula has many medicinal parts, such as its flowers, which contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, and its leaves, which are rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The roots of Prunus × lannesiana f. plenapendula are a source of saponins and phenolic acids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant's fruits, although not specifically mentioned in literature, may contain various nutrients and compounds, although specific details are lacking. The bark of Prunus × lannesiana f. plenapendula contains tannins, which are known for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties.
side_effects: Prunus × lannesiana f. plenapendula can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal issues due to the presence of saponins and other compounds. Ingesting large quantities of the plant's flowers or leaves can lead to vomiting, diarrhea, and stomach pain. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap or leaves can cause contact dermatitis, characterized by redness, itching, and blistering of the skin. Improper use of Prunus × lannesiana f. plenapendula can also interact with certain medications, exacerbating conditions such as diabetes, high blood pressure, or thyroid disorders.
